PERI History — Jan to Jul 2026
KC_03 Mining Blockade Tracker — PERU UNIQUE KILL CONDITION (weight=0.30)
Why KC_03 is Peru's Defining Risk — Invisible in BCCh Chile Canary

Peru has 100+ socio-environmental conflicts annually. Unlike Chile, where copper risk is primarily price-driven (LME copper below $3.20/lb = CLKRI single trigger), Peru's copper disruption mechanism is community-driven road blockades that physically halt mine access — a direct supply disruption that copper price alone cannot capture.

Las Bambas (MMG/China-operated, ~420kt/yr) has been blockaded 20+ times since 2016. Communities in Apurimac, Cusco, and Puno regions block the access road (often Cunahuanca corridor) to extract compensation, job guarantees, or environmental remediation. A single 30-day Las Bambas blockade removes ~35kt of copper from global supply — equivalent to 1.1% of Peru's annual output.

The Tintaya (Glencore) and Quellaveco (Anglo American) mines face similar community pressure. When Las Bambas and Cerro Verde (FCX) are simultaneously blockaded for >30 days, KC_03 reaches 0.90 — triggering ELEVATED composite PERI.

YearEst. Major EventsIntensityKey Context
2019 14 ███████░░░░░ Las Bambas suspended 3 times; Antamina protests
2020 8 ████░░░░░░░░ COVID dampens protests; Las Bambas reduced activity
2021 12 ██████░░░░░░ Castillo election; mining community expectations rise
2022 18 █████████░░░ Castillo impeached; government vacuum; blockades surge
2023 22 ███████████░ Boluarte protests nationwide; Las Bambas 5+ blockades
2024 20 ██████████░░ Sustained community tension; Quellaveco + Las Bambas
2025 17 ████████░░░░ Ongoing Cunahuanca/Tintaya flash points
2026 11 █████░░░░░░░ YTD — Las Bambas tension; Jul situation monitoring
Q3 2026 Status: WATCH — Ongoing Las Bambas community tension; access road monitoring active. Cunahuanca flash point elevated.
PERI Kill Conditions — Full Breakdown
KC Description Weight Score Zone PERI Contrib.
KC_01 Copper Price Shock — Peru 2nd largest producer (11% global). Key mines: Cerro Verde (FCX), Las Bambas (MMG), Quellaveco (Anglo American) 25% 0.3500 WATCH 0.0875
KC_02 Sol/PEN Depreciation — USDPEN ~3.75; elevated stress threshold 4.00; BCRP intervention active 20% 0.3000 WATCH 0.0600
KC_03 Mining Community Blockades — UNIQUE PERU KILL CONDITION. Las Bambas blockaded 20+ times. 100+ socio-environmental conflicts/year. Road blockades halt production — invisible in BCCh Chile canary. PERU UNIQUE 30% 0.5000 WATCH 0.1500
KC_04 Political Crisis — 5 presidents since 2018. Boluarte 5-10% approval. Castillo impeached Dec 2022. Highest constitutional fragility in LatAm after Argentina. 15% 0.4500 WATCH 0.0675
KC_05 El Niño Climate Risk — La Niña conditions 2026. Peru coast = El Niño ground zero. 2023 El Niño disrupted Q1 2024. Anchovy/fishmeal supply chain risk. 10% 0.2000 NORMAL 0.0200
Copper Intelligence — Peru vs Global Signal
Las Bambas (MMG/China)
~420 kt/yr
Blockaded 20+ times since 2016. Community access control = KC_03 driver.
Cerro Verde (Freeport-McMoRan)
~480 kt/yr
FCX direct exposure. Strike risk KC_01. Near Arequipa; less blockade-prone than Las Bambas.
Quellaveco (Anglo American)
~300 kt/yr
NGLOY ADR exposure. Started production 2022. Community agreements being tested.
Antamina (BHP/Glencore JV)
~450 kt/yr
Zinc-copper mine. More stable community relations. BHP ADR indirect exposure.
Sol/PEN Macro & Presidential Instability Timeline
5 Presidents Since 2018 — LatAm Constitutional Fragility Record
PresidentPeriodExit ReasonUSDPEN at Transition

Martín Vizcarra2018–2020Congressional impeachment3.62

Dina Boluarte2022–presentOngoing; 5–10% approval; 60+ deaths 2023 protests3.75

Ticker Name Direction Severity Transmission Note

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2200  NORMAL

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Panama Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.30).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, industrial machinery, consumer electronics, pharmaceuticals. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — copper, gold, silver, zinc — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2200 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Panama Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0800  NORMAL

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As an emerging market, Peru real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Peru are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0800 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3500  WATCH

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Earthquake, Flood, Drought Heatwave. High-severity events (Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Peru is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3500 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.2700WATCH
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.1500NORMAL

SCRI  0.2200  NORMAL

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Panama Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.30).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, industrial machinery, consumer electronics, pharmaceuticals. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — copper, gold, silver, zinc — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2200 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Panama Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0800  NORMAL

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As an emerging market, Peru real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Peru are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0800 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3500  WATCH

Banco Central de Reserva del Perú faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Earthquake, Flood, Drought Heatwave. High-severity events (Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Peru is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3500 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.2700WATCH
